TypeSignature.Value: .class public interface auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit IAuthenticationOperation3 implements class Microsoft.Identity.Client.AuthScheme.IAuthenticationOperation, class Microsoft.Identity.Client.AuthScheme.IAuthenticationOperation2
type IAuthenticationOperation3 = interface interface IAuthenticationOperation2 interface IAuthenticationOperation

Docs.summary: Extends with a lifecycle hook so MSAL can pass runtime context (e.g., the mTLS certificate selected for the current request) to a custom authentication operation. Enables composition of schemes such as CDT + mTLS PoP without MSAL having to replace the operation.

remarks.para: This callback fires on every ExecuteAsync invocation, including when a valid token is served from the cache. Implementations should avoid expensive work that is unnecessary on cache hits.

Thread safety: If the same instance is shared across concurrent ExecuteAsync calls, will be invoked concurrently. Implementations must either be thread-safe or create a fresh instance per ExecuteAsync call (recommended).
Scope: This hook fires only on confidential client token requests. Managed identity flows do not invoke it.
Exceptions: Exceptions thrown from this callback will propagate to the caller of ExecuteAsync without wrapping.

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Threading.Tasks.Task AfterCredentialEvaluationAsync (Microsoft.Identity.Client.AuthScheme.CredentialEvaluationContext context, System.Threading.CancellationToken cancellationToken = default);

.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Threading.Tasks.Task AfterCredentialEvaluationAsync(class Microsoft.Identity.Client.AuthScheme.CredentialEvaluationContext context, valuetype System.Threading.CancellationToken cancellationToken) cil managed
Public Function AfterCredentialEvaluationAsync (context As CredentialEvaluationContext, Optional cancellationToken As CancellationToken = Nothing) As Task

Docs.param: Runtime state owned by MSAL. Never null. Must not be retained past this call.
Docs.summary: MSAL invokes this once per token request, after it has evaluated the credentials that will be used. The operation reads what it needs from and configures itself for the upcoming call.
